alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services with two promotions in Strategy and Operations, including scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs; demonstrated program ownership and cross-functional execution.
alignments.job_requirement: Experience with technical project delivery - managing scope, timelines and measurable outcomes, translating complex concepts into actionable solutions.

alignments.candidate_evidence: Global commercial operations experience at scale with stakeholder engagement and transformation program leadership.
alignments.job_requirement: Experience working with enterprise clients and managing conflicts across a broad stakeholder range

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product operations ownership includes requirements definition, logic design, and UAT—typical of roles requiring documentation and technical communication.

gaps.gap: Extensive hands-on production software engineering, data engineering, and distributed systems experience required; candidate background is strategy and operations, not engineering-delivery.
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has hands-on LLM agentic tooling design/direction and basic Python (actively learning), plus advanced SQL/BigQuery querying—sufficient only if role would accept a non-traditional engineering hire. FDE role description emphasizes 'hands-on builder' and 'implement end-to-end systems,' making this gap substantial.

gaps.gap: Deep experience with Apache Spark, distributed computing, and Spark runtime internals required; candidate has no stated Spark or distributed systems experience.
gaps.mitigation: Spark expertise is foundational for FDE role at Databricks. No ramp time mentioned in JD; gap is not easily closed in onboarding.

gaps.gap: Working knowledge of two or more Cloud ecosystems (AWS, Azure, GCP) and expertise in at least one; candidate cloud experience not mentioned.
gaps.mitigation: Cloud platform experience is material for FDE delivery. No evidence in candidate profile.

gaps.gap: MLOps, ML/AI models, and AI APIs production deployment experience; candidate exposure limited to agentic tooling design and basic Python learning.
gaps.mitigation: Candidate's agentic tooling work is design and direction, not production MLOps engineering. Gap remains substantial.

gaps.gap: Role family mismatch: Forward Deployed Engineer is an implementation-heavy engineering delivery role, not a strategy/operations or transformation-strategy role.
gaps.mitigation: Approved stretch includes 'Implementation-heavy AI transformation roles where business problem solving, stakeholder management, process design, and execution outweigh production engineering'—but FDE role emphasizes production engineering, architecture decisions, and hands-on code/implementation as core deliverables, not outweighed by business/strategy.

hard_blockers.evidence: JD states 'Extensive experience in data engineering, data platforms & analytics, or software engineering' and 'Lead impactful customer technical projects by delivering production-grade systems, designing and building reference architectures, custom applications.' Candidate background is strategy and operations at Google with no stated data engineering, analytics platform, or production software-engineering experience. Hands-on LLM agentic tooling direction and basic Python learning do not constitute extensive production engineering or data platform experience required for FDE role.

JD requires 'Deep experience with distributed computing with Apache Spark™ and knowledge of Spark runtime internals.' Candidate profile includes no mention of Spark, distributed computing, or big-data systems engineering. This is a foundational technical requirement for Databricks FDE role.

JD emphasizes 'hands-on, customer-facing role for builders' and 'implement end-to-end systems spanning data engineering, AI, and application development' with 'Production Solution Delivery' as the first impact statement. Candidate is a strategy and operations leader, not an engineering builder or implementer. Honest gaps include 'No production software-engineering background' and product experience is 'requirements, logic design, UAT, rollout, and transformation rather than native engineering product management.'

output.level_rationale: FDE role at Databricks requires extensive hands-on engineering experience (data engineering, software engineering, distributed systems). deep Spark and cloud expertise. production MLOps and AI API deployment. and customer technical delivery at scale. JD emphasizes 'hands-on builder' and 'implement end-to-end systems.' These requirements map to L5 IC engineering or early L6 delivery leadership. No reporting line or company-size context stated. confidence is moderate.
output.summary: This is a Senior Forward Deployed Engineer role—a hands-on, customer-facing engineering-delivery position requiring extensive data engineering, software engineering, and Apache Spark expertise. The candidate is a Strategy and Operations leader with eight years at Google, advanced operations and transformation skills, and emerging agentic AI tooling capability, but no production software-engineering, data-engineering, or distributed-systems background. The role's core deliverables are production-grade system implementation, architecture design, and hands-on coding—fundamentally misaligned with candidate's strategy-operations profile. Hard blockers on extensive engineering experience, Spark expertise, and hands-on builder scope make this a skip.
uncertainties: Company stage and team size not stated; assumes mid-stage or mature given Databricks' market position, but cannot confirm seniority anchors from JD alone.
Databricks certification mentioned as a requirement; not specified if it is prerequisite or obtainable post-hire.

Candidate's agentic tooling work involves directing LLM agents to build systems—unclear whether this constitutes sufficient 'hands-on engineering' signal for a non-traditional FDE path; JD emphasizes direct implementation and Spark expertise as core, not alternatives.
